prompt:
Goal: Create an elegant Chinese guofeng morning-greeting poster themed around lotus and calm daily ritual, with collectible book-cover-level typography and a refined ink-wash aesthetic.

Canvas: Wide horizontal 16:9 poster, warm ivory rice-paper background with subtle fiber texture, generous empty space in the center-left, delicate muted colors.

Layout: Left third contains all calendar and greeting typography; right half is a large botanical lotus illustration. Keep the composition asymmetrical and airy. On the far left, place a narrow vertical date/weather column. Just right of it, place the main title in large black vertical Chinese calligraphy. To the right of the main title, place a thin vertical red divider line and a small vertical blessing quote. Along the lower left, place two horizontal recommendation rows with small circular seals.

Text content: Use these exact visible Chinese text elements unless customized: main vertical title 荷风早安; date column shows "2026 / 06 / 26" and "星期五"; weather column shows a small cloud-rain icon, "长沙天气｜阴有阵雨", and "25°C｜30°C"; vertical quote 早安，愿你今日心有清荷，步步生香。; bottom row 1 has a green circular seal "宜" followed by "今日宜｜赏荷、读书、慢行、见想见的人"; bottom row 2 has a red circular seal "忌" followed by "今日忌｜烦躁、拖延、久坐、过度忧心". Add a small red square seal stamp near the quote.

Botanical subject details: Right side contains exactly 5 main lotus elements: 1 large pale pink open lotus blossom at upper-right center, 1 smaller pale pink lotus blossom/bud near upper middle, 1 upright pink bud near top center, and 2 dark lotus seed pods on curved stems. Add exactly 6 large lotus leaves in desaturated deep green and gray-green tones, with visible white veins and distressed ink texture. Include several thin stems, small reddish berry-like branch accents, scattered ink dots, and faint flowing water lines in the middle.

Visual style: Chinese ink painting mixed with modern editorial poster design, muted sage green, charcoal black, pale rose pink, cinnabar red accents, off-white paper. Typography should be vertical, refined, high-end, and balanced, with expressive brush calligraphy for the title and delicate Song/Ming-style printed Chinese for the smaller text.

Constraints: No people, no photographic realism, no extra logos or watermarks, preserve large negative space, keep text sharp and legible, maintain a serene morning mood.
